description: Disabling lockfile integrity or hash verification to get a failing install to succeed, masking a tampered or drifted package.
body: Failure mode: an install fails on a hash or version mismatch and the fix applied is to regenerate the lockfile or pass a flag that skips integrity checks, so a package whose content no longer matches what was reviewed installs anyway. Detection signals: integrity-check flags added to install commands; lockfile regenerated to clear a mismatch; resolved content differs from the reviewed version. Do not bypass integrity verification to unblock a build. Safer fallback: investigate why the hash changed, confirm the package's legitimacy from its source, and only update the lockfile after verifying the new content. Risk boundary: treat a hash mismatch as potential tampering and escalate rather than disabling the check.
